issuerType: SEC-registered Delaware statutory trust; diversified open-end management investment company
legalStructure: Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund is a diversified open-end management investment company, commonly called a mutual fund, organized as a series of Franklin Templeton Trust, a Delaware statutory trust registered with the SEC. The Fund is intended to be a government money market fund under Rule 2a-7 and has elected regulated investment company treatment under Subchapter M of the Internal Revenue Code.
managementFee: 0.15% annually of the Fund's average daily net assets
fundAdmin: Franklin Templeton Services, LLC; sub-administrator: JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.
targetInvestors: U.S. individual investors via the Benji App and institutional investors via the Institutional Web Portal; non-Stellar blockchain access is subject to eligibility and approval by the Fund and transfer agent.

metadata.fundName: Franklin OnChain U.S. Government Money Fund
metadata.blockchainRecordkeeping: The transfer agent maintains the official record of share ownership through a proprietary blockchain-integrated system.
